meanings.definition: advanced in years; (`aged' is pronounced as two syllables)
examples: elderly residents could remember the construction of the first skyscraper

meanings.definition: used of the older of two persons of the same name especially used to distinguish a father from his son
(used especially of persons) having lived for a relatively long time or attained a specific age

examples: the once capital of the state
had us a high old time
went upriver to look at a sure-enough fish wheel

meanings.definition: of a very early stage in development
examples: Old English is also called Anglo Saxon
Old High German is High German from the middle of the 9th to the end of the 11th century

meanings.definition: just preceding something else in time or order
